title: Handshake vs Salesflow — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and Salesflow for LinkedIn automation. See features, pricing, safety, and multi-sender support side-by-side to pick the right tool for your team.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a cloud-based LinkedIn automation platform purpose-built for multi-sender rotation. Teams manage unlimited LinkedIn sender accounts from a single dashboard with built-in warmup scheduling, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies included on every plan. Pricing is based on sender accounts — not per-seat — with unlimited team seats included. Plans start at $69/month for 1 sender and scale through Growth ($199/mo for 5 senders) and Scale ($299/mo for 10 senders) tiers.
overview.competitor: Salesflow is a cloud-based LinkedIn automation platform focused on lead generation and sales outreach with multichannel capabilities. Their Single User plan is priced at $99/month per user ($79/mo billed annually) and includes up to 400 connection requests per month, follow-up messaging sequences, open InMail automation, AI-driven lead inbox, CSV imports, and advanced reporting. Salesflow also offers custom-priced Teams and Agency plans with features like blacklisting, white-label branding, admin dashboards, and dedicated IP settings.

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in, core feature — rotate outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts automatically with smart lead distribution
featureComparison.competitor: Not available on Single User plan — Agency plan offers multi-account management but no automatic rotation

featureComparison.handshake: Automated warmup scheduling with gradual ramp-up for new accounts over 3 weeks
featureComparison.competitor: Human behavior mimicking for safety, but no dedicated warmup automation feature

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox for all sender accounts — manage every conversation in one place
featureComparison.competitor: AI-driven lead inbox per account with real-time management and templates

featureComparison.handshake: Unlimited team seats on every plan, shared workspaces and dashboards
featureComparison.competitor: Teams plan with team management features — custom pricing required
LinkedIn + email multichannel outreach included on all plans

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies included on all plans, human-like sending patterns
featureComparison.competitor: Cloud-based with human behavior mimicking; dedicated IP settings on Agency plan
Up to 800 Open InMails per month with LinkedIn Premium/Sales Navigator

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in A/B testing on all plans
featureComparison.competitor: Campaign variations possible but no formal A/B testing feature
AI-driven lead inbox with intelligent prioritization and response suggestions

featureComparison.handshake: Advanced campaign analytics with acceptance rates, reply tracking, sender performance
featureComparison.competitor: Advanced reporting and analytics with detailed campaign statistics
White-label branding available on Agency plan
$495/mo for 5 users ($395/mo annual) on Single User plans
$990/mo for 10 users ($790/mo annual), or custom Agency pricing

sections.handshakeWins:
Handshake's primary advantages over Salesflow are multi-sender rotation, pricing at scale, and included safety infrastructure.

Multi-sender rotation is the defining difference. Salesflow treats each user as an independent account — you get 400 connection requests per month per account, and there's no way to automatically distribute leads across multiple senders. Handshake rotates outreach across all connected sender accounts natively, maximizing your reach while keeping each individual account within safe daily limits.

The pricing difference is substantial and grows with scale. A single sender is $69/mo on Handshake vs. $99/mo on Salesflow. At 5 senders, it's $199/mo vs. $495/mo. At 10 senders, it's $299/mo vs. $990/mo. And Handshake includes unlimited team seats — on Salesflow, every person who needs access to the platform requires their own paid user seat.

Handshake includes premium residential proxies matched to each sender's geographic location on every plan. Salesflow offers dedicated IP settings only on their Agency plan (custom pricing). For teams running standard plans, Salesflow's proxy/IP situation is less transparent.

The unified inbox across all sender accounts is a significant operational advantage for team leads and account managers. Salesflow's AI inbox is per-account — there's no cross-account view for managing conversations across multiple senders.
sections.competitorWins:
Salesflow has notable strengths in multichannel outreach, AI features, and agency white-labeling.

The built-in email outreach on all plans means you can run LinkedIn + email sequences without a separate tool. For teams that want to follow up with prospects across channels, Salesflow provides this natively. Handshake focuses exclusively on LinkedIn.

Salesflow's AI-driven lead inbox is a genuinely useful feature for prioritizing responses and managing high-volume inboxes. The AI helps identify which replies are most likely to convert, saving time on manual inbox management.

For agencies, Salesflow's white-label branding, admin panel for managing unlimited clients, and global activity dashboard are valuable. Handshake doesn't offer white-label capabilities — agencies manage client accounts through Handshake-branded workspaces.

Salesflow's 7-day free trial and established reputation (they've been operating since 2019) also give confidence to teams evaluating the platform.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if you're scaling LinkedIn outreach across multiple accounts and need multi-sender rotation, a unified inbox, and predictable pricing. It's ideal for sales teams running 3+ sender accounts, agencies managing multiple client campaigns, and anyone who prioritizes included residential proxies and safe scaling. If LinkedIn is your primary outbound channel and cost-efficiency at scale matters, Handshake is the clear choice.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Salesflow if you need LinkedIn + email multichannel outreach in a single tool, or if you're an agency that requires white-label branding for client-facing dashboards. Salesflow is also worth considering if the AI-driven inbox prioritization is important to your workflow, or if you're a solo user who values the structured 400-invites-per-month approach.
sections.verdict: Handshake wins decisively on LinkedIn outreach scaling, pricing, and safety infrastructure. At every team size, Handshake is 30-70% cheaper than Salesflow, and the multi-sender rotation and unified inbox give teams dramatically better efficiency. Salesflow holds its own for multichannel sequences (LinkedIn + email) and agency white-labeling. If your outreach is LinkedIn-first, choose Handshake. If you need email in the same tool and agency branding, consider Salesflow.

faqs.question: Is Handshake cheaper than Salesflow?
faqs.answer: Yes, at every scale. For 1 sender, Handshake is $69/mo vs. Salesflow's $99/mo. For 5 senders, it's $199/mo vs. $495/mo. For 10 senders, it's $299/mo vs. $990/mo. Handshake also includes unlimited team seats at no extra cost.

faqs.question: Does Salesflow support multi-sender rotation?
faqs.answer: No. Each Salesflow user operates their own LinkedIn account independently. The Agency plan offers multi-account management, but there's no automatic rotation or lead distribution between accounts.

faqs.question: Can Salesflow do email outreach?
faqs.answer: Yes. Salesflow includes multichannel email outreach on all plans. This is a feature Handshake doesn't offer — Handshake focuses specifically on LinkedIn automation.

faqs.question: Which tool is better for agencies?
faqs.answer: It depends on your priority. Salesflow offers white-label branding and a client admin panel. Handshake offers better pricing (5 senders for $199/mo vs. $495/mo), multi-sender rotation, and a unified inbox. Many agencies choose Handshake for cost efficiency and add their own branding layer.

faqs.question: Does Handshake have an AI inbox like Salesflow?
faqs.answer: Handshake's unified inbox includes smart filtering and reply tracking but doesn't have AI-driven prioritization like Salesflow's AI inbox. However, Handshake's unified view across all sender accounts is a significant advantage that Salesflow doesn't offer.
